sutta: now there are rows in the mind of the venable sariputta as he sat alone in seclusion this reflection if among would abandon unwholesome ways if he would make wholesomeness become whom should he respect whom should he revere and on whom should he rely then taught the variable 1 let among respect revere and rely on the teacher if he would abandon unwholesome ways and make wholesomeness become let him respect revere and rely on dhamma on the sangha on the training on concentration and earnestness let him respect revere and rely on goodwill if he would abandon unwholesome ways and make wholesomeness become then fought he these things in me have been cleared and purified what if i were to go and tell them to the exalted 1 they will become both purified within me and better reconcile for just as a man might come upon a gold ring of pure and sterling quality and thing this ring of mind is a pure and sterling goal but what if i were to go and show it to the goldsmiths my ring of gold passed by them will be both purified and better reckon so even so when i have gone and told the exalted 1 these things in me will be both purified and better reckon so
commentary: i'll just stop here for a moment here the available sarikuta is talking about 7 things that manga should respect and rely on if he wants to abandon a wholesome state state sir and develop wholesome states and the first 1 is the teacher that means the buddha the second is the dhamma and the third is the sangha and the sangha generally means the sangha of the bhikkhu sangha sometimes is also mentioned the aryan sangha but then it's very difficult to know who are the aryans so generally it means the bhikkhu sangha and you can also say it means the big the aryan beku sangha also then the fourth 1 is training this training the last time i spoke on the sutas i mentioned this training as being generally the precepts actually it's made a mistake there there's more to the precepts than just train in training the training generally means uh the whole of the the training in the holy life in the spiritual path that means the various things to cultivate in for example charana in charana there are a list of things to cultivate for example moral conduct and then there is contentment and then there is uh guarding the sixth sense doors and there's moderation in eating and there is uh devotion to wakefulness and then there is uh cultivating the 7 sadhama the 7 excellent things that we just spoke about just now and then uh mindfulness and awareness and um and uh abandonment of the hindrances seclusion attainment of the 4 janus all these part of the training and the training also means to submit oneself to a teacher because when 1 submits oneself to a teacher the teacher guides a person along the holy path some people think that coming into the holy part is just meditation it's not really because the aim of the holy life is to let go let go of our attachments let go of our likes and dislikes finally to let go of our ego and when the person comes into the holy life he does not know all these things are important sometimes like in the forest monasteries in the thailand you are taught to when you go on arms around with the teacher and when you come back and the teachers bowl is full you have to carry the teacher's bowl in addition to your boat and sometimes some monks not only carry 2 balls they even carry 3 bowls and they make it a point to even beg the teacher to give them the ball because they want to submit to their ego to let go of their ego so on and then they carry the teachers both and they come back and when they come back and they get water already and they wash the teachers feet and many monks go and wash the teacher's feet all this is humbling oneself this is all part of the training in the holy life it is not like some people think just come wear the ropes and meditate all the time then just by meditating all the time you cannot let go of a lot of attachments so all these things count is all part of the training and letting go of our likes and dislikes whatever the teacher says we think that he has more experience than us and then we submit ourselves instead of thinking to do what we like to do and we do what the teacher likes us to do and these are all part of the training so now the suita continues then indeed the verbal sariputta in the evening arose from seclusion and approached the exalted 1 and having come he saluted the exalted 1 and sat down at 1 side and said lord as i said alone in seclusion this reflection arose in my mind whom should a man respect whom should he revere and on whom should he rely if he would abandon unwholesome ways and make wholesomeness become and i thought he should respect revere and rely on the teacher dhamma the sangha the training concentration earnestness and goodwill these things are cleared and purified within me but if i were to speak about them to the exalted 1 they would be both purified and better reconciled just as if a man who had to find a gold ring etc and take it to the goldsmith he would be both purified and better reckon so after being passed by them even so these conditions in me etc etc after i have spoken of them to the exalted 1 and the buddha said well done well done sarikuta among should respect revere and rely on the teacher dhamma the sangha the training concentration earnestness and goodwill if he would abandon unwholesome ways and make wholesomeness become when he had thus spoken the verbal sarituta said lord in its fullness i know the meaning of what the exalted 1 has said in brief that among should land veneration for the teacher but should venerate dhamma such a thing is not possible he who lacks veneration for the teacher lacks veneration also for dhamma that among lacking veneration for the teacher and dhamma should venerate sangha is not possible similarly that among lacking veneration for the teacher dhamma and sangha should venerate the training is not possible that among lacking veneration for the teacher dhamma sangha the training should venerate concentration is not possible that among lacking veneration for the teacher and dhamma sangha training concentration should venerate earnestness is not possible that among lacking veneration for the teacher dharma sangha training concentration and earnestness but should venerate goodwill such a thing is not possible he who lacks veneration for the teacher dhamma the sangha training concentration and earnestness lacks veneration also for goodwill so also long in the opposite cases that a monk should venerate the teacher but should not generate dhamma such a thing is not possible etc etc thus lord in its fullness i understand the exalted ones each and the buddha said well done well done sariputta well have you grasped the meaning of what i have said in brief even in its fullness and the exalted 1 confirmed word for word all that the verb sariputta had said adding thus is the meaning of my speech to be regarded so in this suta these things 7 things are important for the for among if he wants to abandon unwholesome states and develop wholesome states but the interesting part here is that the buddha said if a man if a man does not venerate teacher it's not possible that he would venerate the dhamma if he does not venerate the teacher and the dhamma it is not possible that he will venerate the sangha and if he does not venerate the buddha dharma and sangha he will also not venerate the training and if he does not generate all these 4 things and he will also not venerate concentration so you see here the the concentration again is stressing and in the buddha's discourses we always find concentration is always given as a precondition for wisdom that is why concentration is always stressed in the discourses of the buddha the next utah is
